devanagari: उद्धव उवाच । अथ ते तदनुज्ञाता भुक्त्वा पीत्वा च वारुणीम् । तया विभ्रंशितज्ञाना दुरुक्तैर्मर्म पस्पृशुः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: uddhava uvāca atha te tadanujñātā bhuktvā pītvā ca vāruṇīm tayā vibhraṃśitajñānā duruktairmarma paspṛśuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative plural from √ jñā class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ jñā class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ jñā class 3 verb], [nominative plural from √ jñā class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ jñā class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ jñā class 9 verb]

translation:
Uddhava said:
Then, having been permitted by them (Brāhmaṇas), they (Yādavas etc.) drank (the flour-made) liquor. They lost their knowledge (sobriety), and cut each other to the quick by bitter words.

devanagari: तेषां मैरेयदोषेण विषमीकृतचेतसाम् । निम्लोचति रवावासीत् वेणूनामिव मर्दनम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ maireyadoṣeṇa viṣamīkṛtacetasām nimlocati ravāvāsīt veṇūnāmiva mardanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: When their minds became unbalanced and vehement by the evil effect of the wine, the destruction (as a result of fight) among them, after sunset, was like that of bamboos (friction between which creates forest conflagration consuming the forest of bamboos).

devanagari: भगवान् स्वात्ममायाया गतिं तां अवलोक्य सः । सरस्वतीं उपस्पृश्य वृक्षमूलमुपाविशत् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavān svātmamāyāyā gatiṃ tāṃ avalokya saḥ sarasvatīṃ upaspṛśya vṛkṣamūlamupāviśat
translation: Seeing that course of events brought about by his māyā, the Lord sipped water of the Sarasvatī, and sat down at the foot of a tree.

devanagari: अहं चोक्तो भगवता प्रपन्नार्तिहरेण ह । बदरीं त्वं प्रयाहीति स्वकुलं सञ्जिहीर्षुणा ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ cokto bhagavatā prapannārtihareṇa ha badarīṃ tvaṃ prayāhīti svakulaṃ sañjihīrṣuṇā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: And (while we were already at Dvārakā), I was asked to go to Badarī by the Lord who removes afflictions of his devotees, (but) who desired to destroy his own clan.

devanagari: अथापि तदभिप्रेतं जानन् अहं अरिन्दम । पृष्ठतोऽन्वगमं भर्तुः पादविश्लेषणाक्षमः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpi tadabhipretaṃ jānan ahaṃ arindama pṛṣṭhato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jñā class 9 verb], [vocative single from √ jñā class 9 verb]

translation: Oh conquerer of the enemies! Though I understood his intention, I followed him, as I could not bear the separation from the feet of the Master.

devanagari: अद्राक्षमेकमासीनं विचिन्वन् दयितं पतिम् । श्रीनिकेतं सरस्वत्यां कृतकेतमकेतनम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: adrākṣamekamāsīnaṃ vicinvan dayitaṃ patim śrīniketaṃ sarasvatyāṃ kṛtaketamaketanam
translation: Searching for the most beloved Master (who protects by granting the knowledge of the Soul), I found him, the abode of the goddess Lakṣmī, sitting alone on the bank of the Sarasvatī, without any shelter (as he is the shelter of all).

devanagari: श्यामावदातं विरजं प्रशान्तारुणलोचनम् । दोर्भिश्चतुर्भिः विदितं पीतकौशाम्बरेण च ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: śyāmāvadātaṃ virajaṃ praśāntāruṇalocanam dorbhiścaturbhiḥ viditaṃ pītakauśāmbareṇa ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vid class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 2 verb]

translation: (I saw the Lord) of beautiful blue complexion, composed of pure sattva, with calm, reddish eyes, recognisable by his four arms and by yellow silken garments.

devanagari: वाम ऊरौ अवधिश्रित्य दक्षिणाङ्घ्रिसरोरुहम् । अपाश्रितार्भकाश्वत्थं अकृशं त्यक्तपिप्पलम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: vāma ūrau avadhiśritya dakṣiṇāṅghrisaroruham apāśritārbhakāśvatthaṃ akṛśaṃ tyaktapippalam
translation: He was sitting with his lotus-like right foot on the left thigh, leaning against a young Aśvattha tree, with all pleasures of the senses renounced, (yet) in perfect bliss.

devanagari: तस्मिन् महाभागवतो द्वैपायनसुहृत्सखा । लोकान् अनुचरन् सिद्ध आससाद यदृच्छया ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin mahābhāgavato dvaipāyanasuhṛtsakhā lokān anucaran siddha āsasāda yadṛcchayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: In the course of his wandering over the world, there came by chance a siddha (Maitreya), the great devotee of the Lord, and a friend and well wisher ofDvaipāyana (Vyāsa).

devanagari: तस्यानुरक्तस्य मुनेर्मुकुन्दः । प्रमोदभावानतकन्धरस्य । आश्रृण्वतो मां अनुरागहास । समीक्षया विश्रमयन् उवाच ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: tasyānuraktasya munermukundaḥ pramodabhāvānatakandharasya āśrṛṇvato māṃ anurāgahāsa samīkṣayā viśramayan uvāca

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: While the devoted sage, with his neck (head) bending with joy and devotion, was listening, Mukunda (Kṛṣṇa) removing my fatigue with a long smiling look full of love (compassion), said to me:

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । वेदाहमन्तर्मनसीप्सितं ते । ददामि यत्तद् दुरवापमन्यैः । सत्रे पुरा विश्वसृजां वसूनां । मत्सिद्धिकामेन वसो त्वयेष्टः ॥ ११ ...
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca vedāhamantarmanasīpsitaṃ te dadāmi yattad duravāpamanyaiḥ satre purā viśvasṛjāṃ vasūnāṃ matsiddhikāmena vaso tvayeṣṭaḥ

translation:
The Lord said:
Being an antaryāmin (dweller in the hearts of all) I know what you wish. What I give to you is difficult for others to obtain; (for) in days gone by, in the long sacrificial session performed by Prajāpatis and Vasus, Oh (former) Vasu, you performed the sacrifice with a desire to attaining me.

devanagari: स एष साधो चरमो भवानां । आसादितस्ते मदनुग्रहो यत् । यन्मां नृलोकान् रह उत्सृजन्तं । दिष्ट्या ददृश्वान् विशदानुवृत्त्या ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eṣa sādho caramo bhavānāṃ āsāditaste madanugraho yat yanmāṃ nṛlokān raha utsṛjantaṃ diṣṭyā dadṛśvān viśadānuvṛttyā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Oh virtuous one! This is the last of your births, as my grace has been secured by you in the present birth. How glad I am! that you have come with singleminded devotion to see me when I am about to leave this mortal world (to go to Vaikuṇṭha), in this solitary place.

devanagari: पुरा मया प्रोक्तमजाय नाभ्ये । पद्मे निषण्णाय ममादिसर्गे । ज्ञानं परं मन्महिमावभासं । यत्सूरयो भागवतं वदन्ति ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: purā mayā proktamajāya nābhye padme niṣaṇṇāya mamādisarge jñānaṃ paraṃ manmahimāvabhāsaṃ yatsūrayo bhāgavataṃ vadanti

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: Formerly (in Pādma Kalpa), at the beginning of creation, the highest knowledge throwing light on my greatness (majesty)—which the learned ones designated as the Bhāgavata—was imparted by me to the unborn god (Brahmā) seated in the lotus sprouting forth from my navel.

devanagari: इत्यादृतोक्तः परमस्य पुंसः । प्रतिक्षणानुग्रहभाजनोऽहम् । स्नेहोत्थरोमा स्खलिताक्षरस्तं । मुञ्चञ्छुचः प्राञ्जलिराबभाषे ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: ityādṛtoktaḥ paramasya puṃsaḥ pratikṣaṇānugrahabhājano snehottharomā skhalitākṣarastaṃ muñcañchucaḥ prāñjalirābabhāṣe

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: I, who was thus respectfully addressed, and who was the recipient of the favour of a long look of the Supreme Man, with my hair standing on their ends due to my affection (to him), with flowing tears, and bowing with folded hands, addressed to him in faltering words.

devanagari: को न्वीश ते पादसरोजभाजां । सुदुर्लभोऽर्थेषु चतुर्ष्वपीह । तथापि नाहं प्रवृणोमि भूमन् । भवत्पदाम्भोज निषेवणोत्सुकः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: ko nvīśa te pādasarojabhājāṃ sudurlabho tathāpi nāhaṃ pravṛṇomi bhūman bhavatpadāmbhoja niṣevaṇotsukaḥ
translation: “Oh Lord! Out of the four goals of life (viz. dharma, artha, kāma and Mokṣa), which goal is difficult to be achieved by those who resort to your lotus-like feet? But, Oh Almighty, I do not request you for any one of them, as I am eager to serve your lotus-like feet.

devanagari: कर्माण्यनीहस्य भवोऽभवस्य । ते दुर्गाश्रयोऽथारिभयात्पलायनम् । कालात्मनो यत्प्रमदायुताश्रयः । स्वात्मन् रतेः खिद्यति धीर्विदामिह ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: karmāṇyanīhasya bhavo te durgāśrayo kālātmano yatpramadāyutāśrayaḥ svātman rateḥ khidyati dhīrvidāmiha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: That you performed actions, though you are devoid of actions and desire to do them, that you have taken a birth though you are birthless, that you resorted to a castle or fled away through the fear of the enemy, even though the god of Death is your form[1], that you married with ten thousand young women despite your enjoyment of your self-blissfulness—by these the intellect of the learned ones gets confused and fatigued.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
kālātmanaḥ [kālātman]—You are the destroyer of all—Padaratnāvalī.

devanagari: मन्त्रेषु मां वा उपहूय यत्त्वं । अकुण्ठिताखण्डसदात्मबोधः । पृच्छेः प्रभो मुग्ध इवाप्रमत्तः । तन्नो मनो मोहयतीव देव ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: mantreṣu māṃ vā upahūya yattvaṃ akuṇṭhitākhaṇḍasadātmabodhaḥ pṛccheḥ prabho mugdha ivāpramattaḥ tanno mano mohayatīva deva

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative dual from √ muh ], [vocative dual from √ muh ], [accusative dual from √ muh ], [locative single from √ muh ]

translation: Or, Oh Almighty Lord! It throws our mind in confusion that you, whose power of self-knowledge is unobstructed and continuous, should invite me for consultations like an ignorant person, though you are never ignorant or careless.

devanagari: ज्ञानं परं स्वात्मरहःप्रकाशं । प्रोवाच कस्मै भगवान् समग्रम् । अपि क्षमं नो ग्रहणाय भर्तः । वदाञ्जसा यद् वृजिनं तरेम ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: jñānaṃ paraṃ svātmarahaḥprakāśaṃ provāca kasmai bhagavān samagram api kṣamaṃ no grahaṇāya bhartaḥ vadāñjasā yad vṛjinaṃ tarema

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: You have completely disclosed to Brahmā, the supreme knowledge throwing light on your own (essential) mysterious nature. Oh Lord, if it be within our capacity to receive it, explain it, to us so that we may easily cross the misery (of the worldly existence).”

devanagari: इत्यावेदितहार्दाय मह्यं स भगवान् परः । आदिदेश अरविन्दाक्ष आत्मनः परमां स्थितिम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ityāveditahārdāya mahyaṃ sa bhagavān paraḥ ādideśa aravindākṣa ātmanaḥ paramāṃ sthitim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[nominative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ mah ], [accusative single from √ mah ]

translation: To me who have thus expressed the cherished desire of my heart, the Supreme Lord of lotus-like eyes instructed me (gave me insight) into his Supreme Nature.

devanagari: स एवं आराधितपादतीर्थाद् । अधीततत्त्वात्मविबोधमार्गः । प्रणम्य पादौ परिवृत्य देवं । इहागतोऽहं विरहातुरात्मा ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: sa evaṃ ārādhitapādatīrthād adhītatattvātmavibodhamārgaḥ praṇamya pādau parivṛtya devaṃ ihāgato
translation: I, who have thus learnt the path of the special knowledge of the real Soul from Lord Kṛṣṇa, whose holy feet have been worshipped by me, bowed to his feet, circumambulated the Lord and have come here with my heart afflicted with separation (from him).

devanagari: सोऽहं तद्दर्शनाह्लाद वियोगार्तियुतः प्रभो । गमिष्ये दयितं तस्य बदर्याश्रममण्डलम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: so gamiṣye dayitaṃ tasya badaryāśramamaṇḍalam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 9 verb]

translation: I, who am full of delight at the sight of the Lord, and distressed at my separation from him, shall go to the site of the Badarī—hermitage which was liked by him, and where Lord Nārāyaṇa and venerable sage Nara, both of whom confer blessings on the world, perform a mild (non-troubling) but severe penance fora long period (up to the end of Kalpa).

devanagari: यत्र नारायणो देवो नरश्च भगवान् ऋषिः । मृदु तीव्रं तपो दीर्घं तेपाते लोकभावनौ ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra nārāyaṇo devo naraśca bhagavān ṛṣiḥ mṛdu tīvraṃ tapo dīrghaṃ tepāte lokabhāvanau
translation: I, who am full of delight at the sight of the Lord, and distressed at my separation from him, shall go to the site of the Badarī—hermitage which was liked by him, and where Lord Nārāyaṇa and venerable sage Nara, both of whom confer blessings on the world, perform a mild (non-troubling) but severe penance fora long period (up to the end of Kalpa).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति उद्धवाद् उपाकर्ण्य सुहृदां दुःसहं वधम् । ज्ञानेनाशमयत् क्षत्ता शोकं उत्पतितं बुधः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca iti uddhavād upākarṇya suhṛdāṃ duḥsahaṃ vadham jñānenāśamayat kṣattā śokaṃ utpatitaṃ budhaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having thus heard from Uddhava, the unbearable (report of the) death of his friends and relatives, the learned Vidura controlled the explosion of grief by his knowledge.

devanagari: स तं महाभागवतं व्रजन्तं कौरवर्षभः । विश्रम्भाद् अभ्यधत्तेदं मुख्यं कृष्णपरिग्रहे ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa taṃ mahābhāgavataṃ vrajantaṃ kauravarṣabhaḥ viśrambhād abhyadhattedaṃ mukhyaṃ kṛṣṇaparigrahe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: The prominent Kaurava (Vidura), due to his confidence in (Uddhava), the great devotee of Lord Kṛṣṇa, prominent among Kṛṣṇa’s circle of friends and kinsmen, spoke thus to him while he was about to depart.

devanagari: विदुर उवाच । ज्ञानं परं स्वात्मरहःप्रकाशं । यदाह योगेश्वर ईश्वरस्ते । वक्तुं भवान्नोऽर्हति यद्धि विष्णोः । भृत्याः स्वभृत्यार्थकृतश्चरन्...
english_devnagari: vidura uvāca jñānaṃ paraṃ svātmarahaḥprakāśaṃ yadāha yogeśvara īśvaraste vaktuṃ bhavānno bhṛtyāḥ svabhṛtyārthakṛtaścaranti

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
Vidura said:
Your honour deserves to convey to me the supreme knowledge shedding light on the mysterious nature of the Lord—the knowledge which the Lord of Yoga, the ruler (of the universe) imparted to you. For, the servants of Viṣṇu move about to serve the needs of their devotees.

devanagari: उद्धव उवाच । ननु ते तत्त्वसंराध्य ऋषिः कौषारवोऽन्ति मे । साक्षाद् भगवतादिष्टो मर्त्यलोकं जिहासता ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: uddhava uvāca nanu te tattvasaṃrādhya ṛṣiḥ kauṣāravo sākṣād bhagavatādiṣṭo martyalokaṃ jihāsatā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Uddhava said:
The sage Maitreya is to be propitiated by you for this knowledge, as it was in my presence that the Lord himself directed him to do so (i.e. to instruct you) at the time of his departure from the world of mortals.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति सह विदुरेण विश्वमूर्तेः । गुणकथया सुधया प्लावितोरुतापः । क्षणमिव पुलिने यमस्वसुस्तां । समुषित औपगविर्निशां ततोऽगात् ॥...
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca iti saha vidureṇa viśvamūrteḥ guṇakathayā sudhayā plāvitorutāpaḥ kṣaṇamiva puline yamasvasustāṃ samuṣita aupagavirniśāṃ tato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
In this way, in the company of Vidura, Uddhava got his anguish (at the separation of Hari) subsided by the nectar-like conversation about the excellent qualities of the Omnipresent Lord, and spent like a moment the night, on the bank of the Yamunā, and then left (for Badarikāśrama).

devanagari: राजोवाच । निधनमुपगतेषु वृष्णिभोजेषु । अधिरथयूथपयूथपेषु मुख्यः । स तु कथमवशिष्ट उद्धवो । यद्धरिरपि तत्यज आकृतिं त्र्यधीशः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: rājovāca nidhanamupagateṣu vṛṣṇibhojeṣu adhirathayūthapayūthapeṣu mukhyaḥ sa tu kathamavaśiṣṭa uddhavo yaddharirapi tatyaja ākṛtiṃ tryadhīśaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural]

translation:
The King said:
How did Uddhava remain (safe from the Brāhmaṇa’s curse) when Hari who was the chief of the Vṛṣṇis and Bhojas, leaders of the commanders of warriors joined the majority, and who (Hari) was the Lord of the three (gods, e.g. Brahmā and others) gave up his mortal coil?

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । ब्रह्मशापापदेशेन कालेनामोघवाञ्छितः । संहृत्य स्वकुलं स्फीतं त्यक्ष्यन् देहमचिन्तयत् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca brahmaśāpāpadeśena kālenāmoghavāñchitaḥ saṃhṛtya svakulaṃ sphītaṃ tyakṣyan dehamacintayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ sphā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sphā class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
The Lord whose will is always supreme (lit. never futile), began to think when he annihilated his own race by the Time (god of death) in the form of a Brāhmaṇa’s curse, and was about to cast off his body.

devanagari: अस्मात् लोकादुपरते मयि ज्ञानं मदाश्रयम् । अर्हत्युद्धव एवाद्धा सम्प्रत्यात्मवतां वरः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: asmāt lokāduparate mayi jñānaṃ madāśrayam arhatyuddhava evāddhā sampratyātmavatāṃ varaḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
The Lord whose will is always supreme (lit. never futile), began to think when he annihilated his own race by the Time (god of death) in the form of a Brāhmaṇa’s curse, and was about to cast off his body.
“Now when I am no more in this world, only Uddhava, the best among those who have realised the soul, deserves to receive the knowledge that is within me (or which relates tome).

devanagari: नोद्धवोऽण्वपि मन्न्यूनो यद्‍गुणैर्नार्दितः प्रभुः । अतो मद्वयुनं लोकं ग्राहयन् इह तिष्ठतु ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: noddhavo ato madvayunaṃ lokaṃ grāhayan iha tiṣṭhatu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ grah ], [vocative single from √ grah ]

translation: Uddhava is not a jot inferior to me. He is the master (of himself) unpurturbed by worldly objects (guṇas). Hence let him remain (after the annihilation of the Yāḍavas) to make people receive knowledge about me.

devanagari: एवं त्रिलोकगुरुणा सन्दिष्टः शब्दयोनिना । बदर्याश्रममासाद्य हरिमीजे समाधिना ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ trilokaguruṇā sandiṣṭaḥ śabdayoninā badaryāśramamāsādya harimīje samādhinā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Thus directed by the lord of the three worlds, the source of the Vedas, (Uddhava) went to the Badarī hermitage and worshipped Hari by deep meditation.

devanagari: विदुरोऽप्युद्धवात् श्रुत्वा कृष्णस्य परमात्मनः । क्रीडयोपात्तदेहस्य कर्माणि श्लाघितानि च ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: viduro krīḍayopāttadehasya karmāṇi ślāghitāni ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ślāgh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ślāgh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ ślāgh class 1 verb]

translation: Having heard from Uddhava praiseworthy acts of Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Souḥ who out of sport assumed the human body, and of his laying down the body (death) which increased the courage of the wise, and which is more difficult to understand on the part of beasts (beastlike persons) of unsteady minds and of his being seen mentally by Kṛṣṇa, Oh great Kuru, Vidura meditated upon him, but after the departure of Uddhava, became overcome with feelings of love and wept.

devanagari: देहन्यासं च तस्यैवं धीराणां धैर्यवर्धनम् । अन्येषां दुष्करतरं पशूनां विक्लवात्मनाम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: dehanyāsaṃ ca tasyaivaṃ dhīrāṇāṃ dhairyavardhanam anyeṣāṃ duṣkarataraṃ paśūnāṃ viklavātmanām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single], [imperative passive first single]

translation: Having heard from Uddhava praiseworthy acts of Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Souḥ who out of sport assumed the human body, and of his laying down the body (death) which increased the courage of the wise, and which is more difficult to understand on the part of beasts (beastlike persons) of unsteady minds and of his being seen mentally by Kṛṣṇa, Oh great Kuru, Vidura meditated upon him, but after the departure of Uddhava, became overcome with feelings of love and wept.

devanagari: आत्मानं च कुरुश्रेष्ठ कृष्णेन मनसेक्षितम् । ध्यायन् गते भागवते रुरोद प्रेमविह्वलः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmānaṃ ca kuruśreṣṭha kṛṣṇena manasekṣitam dhyāyan gate bhāgavate ruroda premavihvalaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ īkṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ īkṣ class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having heard from Uddhava praiseworthy acts of Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Souḥ who out of sport assumed the human body, and of his laying down the body (death) which increased the courage of the wise, and which is more difficult to understand on the part of beasts (beastlike persons) of unsteady minds and of his being seen mentally by Kṛṣṇa, Oh great Kuru, Vidura meditated upon him, but after the departure of Uddhava, became overcome with feelings of love and wept.

devanagari: कालिन्द्याः कतिभिः सिद्ध अहोभिर्भरतर्षभ । प्रापद्यत स्वःसरितं यत्र मित्रासुतो मुनिः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: kālindyāḥ katibhiḥ siddha ahobhirbharatarṣabha prāpadyata svaḥsaritaṃ yatra mitrāsuto muniḥ
translation:
From the bank of the Yamunā, Vidura, the Siddha, reached the heavenly river (the Gaṅgā) after some (several days, where Maitreya (the son of Mitrā) was met by him.
